guidelines.title: Pediatric Weight-Based Medication Dosing Guidelines
guidelines.content:
PEDIATRIC WEIGHT-BASED MEDICATION DOSING GUIDELINES

ANALGESICS/ANTIPYRETICS:

Acetaminophen:
- Oral/Rectal: 10-15 mg/kg/dose every 4-6 hours
- Maximum single dose: 15 mg/kg (up to 1000 mg)
- Maximum daily: 75 mg/kg/day (up to 4000 mg/day)
- Infant drops (80 mg/0.8 mL): Use weight-based dosing
- Children's suspension (160 mg/5 mL): Common formulation

Ibuprofen:
- Oral: 5-10 mg/kg/dose every 6-8 hours
- Maximum single dose: 10 mg/kg (up to 400 mg)
- Maximum daily: 40 mg/kg/day (up to 1200 mg/day)
- Not recommended <6 months
- Infant drops (50 mg/1.25 mL) or suspension (100 mg/5 mL)

Naproxen:
- 5-7 mg/kg/dose every 8-12 hours
- Maximum: 1000 mg/day
- Not recommended <2 years

ANTIBIOTICS:

Amoxicillin:
- Standard: 25-50 mg/kg/day divided every 8 hours
- High-dose (AOM, pneumonia): 80-90 mg/kg/day divided every 8-12 hours
- Maximum: 3000 mg/day
- Strep pharyngitis: 50 mg/kg once daily (max 1000 mg) x10 days

Amoxicillin-Clavulanate:
- Standard: 25-45 mg/kg/day (amox component) divided every 12 hours
- High-dose: 80-90 mg/kg/day divided every 12 hours
- Use 7:1 ratio formulation for high-dose
- Maximum: 3000 mg/day amoxicillin

Azithromycin:
- Otitis media: 10 mg/kg day 1 (max 500 mg), then 5 mg/kg days 2-5
- Pharyngitis: 12 mg/kg daily x5 days (max 500 mg/day)
- Community-acquired pneumonia: Same as otitis media dosing

Cephalexin:
- 25-50 mg/kg/day divided every 6-8 hours
- Skin infections: 25-50 mg/kg/day
- Maximum: 4000 mg/day

Ceftriaxone:
- Routine infections: 50-75 mg/kg/day once daily or divided BID
- Meningitis: 100 mg/kg/day divided every 12 hours
- Maximum: 4000 mg/day (2000 mg/dose)
- Gonorrhea: 25-50 mg/kg IM (max 125-250 mg)

Trimethoprim-Sulfamethoxazole:
- UTI: 6-12 mg TMP/kg/day divided every 12 hours
- Maximum: 320 mg TMP/day
- Prophylaxis: 2-4 mg TMP/kg once daily

ALLERGY/ASTHMA:

Diphenhydramine:
- 1-1.25 mg/kg/dose every 6 hours
- Maximum: 50 mg/dose, 300 mg/day
- Anaphylaxis adjunct: 1-2 mg/kg IV/IM (max 50 mg)

Cetirizine:
- 6 months-2 years: 2.5 mg once daily
- 2-5 years: 2.5 mg daily, may increase to 5 mg/day
- ≥6 years: 5-10 mg once daily

Loratadine:
- 2-5 years: 5 mg once daily
- ≥6 years: 10 mg once daily

Albuterol (Nebulized):
- <5 years: 2.5 mg (0.5 mL of 0.5%) every 4-6 hours
- ≥5 years: 2.5-5 mg every 4-6 hours
- Acute: Every 20 min x3 doses, then every 1-4 hours
- MDI: 2-8 puffs every 20 min x3 doses (acute)

Prednisolone/Prednisone:
- Asthma flare: 1-2 mg/kg/day (max 60 mg) x3-5 days
- Croup: Dexamethasone 0.6 mg/kg x1 (max 16 mg) preferred

Antihistamines:
- Hydroxyzine: 0.5 mg/kg every 6 hours (max 50 mg)

GI MEDICATIONS:

Ondansetron (Zofran):
- 0.15 mg/kg/dose every 8 hours (max 8 mg)
- Weight-based:
  - 8-15 kg: 2 mg
  - 15-30 kg: 4 mg
  - >30 kg: 8 mg
- ODT available for vomiting patients

Omeprazole:
- 1-4 mg/kg/day (usual 0.7-3.3 mg/kg/day)
- 10-20 kg: 10 mg daily
- >20 kg: 20 mg daily
- Erosive esophagitis may need higher doses

Polyethylene Glycol (Miralax):
- 0.5-1.5 g/kg/day (typical: 0.8-1 g/kg/day)
- Disimpaction: 1-1.5 g/kg/day x3 days
- Maintenance: 0.4-0.8 g/kg/day
- 17 g = 1 capful = 1 heaping tablespoon

SEDATION:

Midazolam:
- Procedural sedation: 0.05-0.1 mg/kg IV (max 2 mg/dose)
- Intranasal: 0.2-0.4 mg/kg (max 10 mg)
- Oral: 0.25-0.5 mg/kg (max 20 mg)

Ketamine:
- IV: 1-2 mg/kg over 1-2 min
- IM: 4-5 mg/kg
- Onset: IV 1-2 min, IM 3-5 min
- Duration: 10-20 min
- Contraindicated: <3 months, increased ICP

PROPOFOL:
- 1-2 mg/kg IV bolus
- Infusion: 100-300 mcg/kg/min
- Brief procedures only in ED

EMERGENCY MEDICATIONS:

Epinephrine (Anaphylaxis):
- IM: 0.01 mg/kg of 1:1000 (0.01 mL/kg)
- Maximum: 0.3 mg (<30 kg), 0.5 mg (≥30 kg)
- Auto-injectors: Jr 0.15 mg (<30 kg), 0.3 mg (≥30 kg)
- May repeat every 5-15 min

Epinephrine (Cardiac Arrest):
- 0.01 mg/kg (0.1 mL/kg of 1:10,000) IV/IO
- Maximum: 1 mg
- Repeat every 3-5 minutes

Atropine:
- Bradycardia: 0.02 mg/kg IV/IO
- Minimum dose: 0.1 mg
- Maximum: 0.5 mg (child), 1 mg (adolescent)

Adenosine:
- First dose: 0.1 mg/kg (max 6 mg) rapid IV push
- Second dose: 0.2 mg/kg (max 12 mg)
- Immediate flush with 5-10 mL NS

Dextrose (Hypoglycemia):
- D10: 5 mL/kg IV (0.5 g/kg)
- D25: 2-4 mL/kg IV (0.5-1 g/kg) - NOT for neonates
- D50: 1-2 mL/kg IV - adolescents only

EVIDENCE LEVEL: Level I (AAP, Harriet Lane Handbook)

guidelines.title: Pediatric Vaccine Schedule and Dosing
guidelines.content:
PEDIATRIC VACCINE SCHEDULE AND DOSING

ROUTINE CHILDHOOD VACCINES:

Hepatitis B (HepB):
- Dose: 0.5 mL IM (pediatric), 1.0 mL IM (adult)
- Schedule: Birth, 1-2 months, 6-18 months
- Minimum interval: Dose 1-2: 4 weeks; Dose 2-3: 8 weeks AND 16 weeks after dose 1
- Birth dose within 24 hours of birth

Rotavirus (RV):
- Rotarix (RV1): 2 doses at 2 and 4 months (1 mL oral each)
- RotaTeq (RV5): 3 doses at 2, 4, and 6 months (2 mL oral each)
- First dose: 6-14 weeks 6 days (strict upper limit)
- Last dose: By 8 months 0 days
- Minimum interval: 4 weeks

DTaP (Diphtheria, Tetanus, Pertussis):
- Dose: 0.5 mL IM
- Schedule: 2, 4, 6, 15-18 months, 4-6 years
- 5 doses total before age 7
- Minimum intervals: Dose 1-2-3: 4 weeks; Dose 3-4: 6 months; Dose 4-5: 6 months

Haemophilus influenzae type b (Hib):
- Dose: 0.5 mL IM
- Schedule depends on brand:
  - ActHIB, Hiberix: 2, 4, 6, 12-15 months (4 doses)
  - PedvaxHIB: 2, 4, 12-15 months (3 doses)
- Minimum intervals: 4-8 weeks depending on brand

Pneumococcal Conjugate (PCV15 or PCV20):
- Dose: 0.5 mL IM
- Schedule: 2, 4, 6, 12-15 months
- Minimum intervals: Dose 1-2-3: 4 weeks; Dose 3-4: 8 weeks
- Catch-up: 1-2 doses depending on age started

Inactivated Poliovirus (IPV):
- Dose: 0.5 mL IM or SC
- Schedule: 2, 4, 6-18 months, 4-6 years
- Minimum intervals: 4 weeks between first 3 doses; 6 months for dose 3-4
- Final dose at ≥4 years and ≥6 months after dose 3

MMR (Measles, Mumps, Rubella):
- Dose: 0.5 mL SC
- Schedule: 12-15 months, 4-6 years
- Minimum interval: 4 weeks
- May give at 6 months for travel (doesn't count toward series)

Varicella:
- Dose: 0.5 mL SC
- Schedule: 12-15 months, 4-6 years
- Minimum interval: 3 months (12 weeks if <13 years; 4 weeks if ≥13 years)

Hepatitis A (HepA):
- Dose: 0.5 mL IM (pediatric), 1.0 mL IM (adult)
- Schedule: 12-23 months (dose 1), 6-18 months later (dose 2)
- 2-dose series, minimum interval 6 months

Meningococcal ACWY (MenACWY):
- Dose: 0.5 mL IM
- Schedule: 11-12 years, booster at 16 years
- High-risk: May start at 2 months (Menveo) or 9 months (Menactra)

Meningococcal B (MenB):
- Bexsero: 0.5 mL IM x2 doses (0, ≥1 month)
- Trumenba: 0.5 mL IM x2 doses (0, 6 months) or x3 doses (0, 1-2, 6 months)
- Recommended 16-23 years (preferably 16-18)

HPV:
- Dose: 0.5 mL IM
- Ages 9-14: 2 doses (0, 6-12 months)
- Ages 15-26: 3 doses (0, 1-2, 6 months)
- Same schedule regardless of sex

Influenza (Annual):
- Age 6-35 months: 0.25 mL or 0.5 mL IM (product dependent)
- Age ≥3 years: 0.5 mL IM or intranasal (LAIV)
- First season <9 years: 2 doses 4 weeks apart
- Annually thereafter

COVID-19:
- Follow current CDC recommendations (evolving)
- Formulation and schedule vary by age and product

CATCH-UP PRINCIPLES:
- No restart needed - continue from where left off
- Minimum intervals between doses apply
- Multiple vaccines can be given same visit (different sites)
- Live vaccines (MMR, varicella, LAIV): Same day or 4 weeks apart

CONTRAINDICATIONS:
- Anaphylaxis to vaccine or component
- Encephalopathy within 7 days of pertussis vaccine (omit pertussis)
- Severe immunodeficiency (live vaccines)
- Pregnancy (live vaccines)

SPECIAL POPULATIONS:

Preterm Infants:
- Vaccinate based on chronological age, not adjusted age
- Same doses as term infants
- May need hepatitis B immunoglobulin if mother HBsAg+

Immunocompromised:
- No live vaccines (MMR, varicella, LAIV, rotavirus)
- May need additional doses of inactivated vaccines
- PCV and PPSV23 per specific guidelines

EVIDENCE LEVEL: Level I (ACIP/CDC Immunization Schedule)
